summary.headline: Centre vs states: the standards map is shifting
summary.spoken: Here is your brief in twenty seconds. 9 items today across the desks, 2 flagged for action. The big one: From 16 Mar 2026 no fresh project-approval applications are accepted at construction/pre-construction stage. And the thread to watch is the centre versus the states on mandatory registration.
highlights: From 16 Mar 2026 no fresh project-approval applications are accepted at construction/pre-construction stage.
Mandatory UTDB registration for hotels, homestays, B&Bs, agents & adventure ops; 5-year validity; homestay classification built in.

deepdive.head: The centre is going voluntary while the states go mandatory
deepdive.intro: NIDHI+ is a voluntary national scheme — and the Ministry has just narrowed it by ending project approvals. But several states are moving the opposite way: making registration compulsory, and in Uttarakhand's case running their own classification. Here's where each stands.
deepdive.take: The risk is NIDHI+ becoming one registry among many. The opportunity is to position it as the interoperable national spine that state systems plug into — a strong line for your reform note.

states.name: Centre · NIDHI+ (MoT)
states.note: Voluntary star classification; project approvals for under-construction units stopped 16 March 2026.

states.name: Uttarakhand
states.note: 2026 Rules: one mandatory framework for every unit, with homestay and B&B classification built in.

states.name: Rajasthan
states.note: Homestay Scheme 2026: fast digital approval, rooms raised 5 to 8, owner-residence rule dropped.

states.name: Uttar Pradesh
states.note: First dedicated state homestay/B&B policy; local-body NOC now mandatory on top of NIDHI+ listing.

states.name: Meghalaya
states.note: Mandatory guest registration via the state tourism app, driven by visitor-safety concerns.

items.title: MoT discontinues Voluntary Scheme of Project Approvals for under-construction hospitality units
items.sowhat: From 16 Mar 2026 no fresh project-approval applications are accepted at construction/pre-construction stage.
items.desk_line: Directly changes NIDHI+ intake; a concrete line for your Secretary reform briefing and work-order narrative.

items.title: Ministry tells Lok Sabha NIDHI+ is the single online system for classification & TSP recognition
items.sowhat: MoT frames NIDHI+ as the system of record for classifying accommodation units and recognising service providers.
items.desk_line: Useful framing language for positioning NIDHI+ value and the unpaid work-order case.

items.title: Uttarakhand notifies Tourism & Travel Business Registration Rules 2026 — one framework for all units
items.sowhat: Mandatory UTDB registration for hotels, homestays, B&Bs, agents & adventure ops; 5-year validity; homestay classification built in.
items.desk_line: A state building a parallel registration + classification regime beside NIDHI+ — the central-vs-state interface is your core domain.

items.title: Rajasthan Homestay Scheme 2026: single-window digital approval, rooms raised 5→8
items.sowhat: Owner-residence requirement dropped; caretaker model allowed; faster digital registration to grow rural homestays.
items.desk_line: Light-touch state compliance model + the rural/community-tourism livelihood framing you're positioning around.

items.title: UP approves first dedicated Homestay & B&B Policy; mandates local-body NOCs
items.sowhat: Until now UP units registered via NIDHI+; new policy layers local NOCs and state incentives on top.
items.desk_line: Shows states layering local compliance over NIDHI+ — relevant to SAATHI self-cert scope.

items.title: Meghalaya makes guest registration via state tourism app compulsory
items.sowhat: Safety-driven mandatory digital guest registration across homestays, resorts and landlords.
items.desk_line: Compliance-as-safety angle; a state digital guest registry that parallels NIDHI's data role.

items.title: WTTC 'Hotel Sustainability Basics' — 12 minimum actions — emerge as a global benchmark
items.sowhat: An achievable entry-level floor that corporate buyers, regulators and investors increasingly expect.
items.desk_line: A minimum-floor model you could reference for an ESG-linked baseline tier in SAATHI / classification.

items.title: GSTC positioned as the 'standard for standards'; OTAs start prioritising certified properties
items.sowhat: GSTC accredits certifiers rather than certifying hotels directly; Booking.com/Google Travel surface certified stays.
items.desk_line: Accreditation-of-certifiers architecture is a model for how QCI/NIDHI+ could position; ties to your ESG track.

items.title: UN Tourism backing national hotel-classification updates: 'business-friendly, future-oriented, guest-centric'
items.sowhat: A 3-year programme to redeploy revised, more credible and guest-focused classification schemes.
items.desk_line: External validation + benchmark language for any move to modernise India's classification criteria.
